message.content:
Complete WAVE 1: Voice Model Migration for ConvergioEdu.

OBJECTIVE: Migrate from gpt-4o-realtime to gpt-realtime-mini for 80-90% cost savings.

TASKS from MasterPlan:
1.1: The Azure deployment is already done (gpt-4o-mini-realtime). We need to update the code.

1.2: Add env var support for AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI
   - Check .env.example and add the new var
   - Default should be 'gpt-4o-mini-realtime'

1.3: Modify realtime-proxy.ts (likely in src/app/api/realtime/ or similar):
   - Add logic to use MINI model by default
   - Keep PREMIUM model (gpt-4o-realtime) for MirrorBuddy only
   - MirrorBuddy needs premium for emotional detection quality

1.4-1.7: These are deployment/monitoring tasks - skip for now

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Find the realtime API route (search for realtime, token, or websocket)
2. Find where the deployment name is configured
3. Add env var AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI
4. Modify code to:
   - Use mini model by default for all maestri/coaches
   - Use premium model only when characterType === 'buddy' (MirrorBuddy)

5. Update .env.example with the new variable
6. Run typecheck and lint

DO NOT ask questions. Implement based on existing code patterns.

file.content:
# ============================================================================
# CONVERGIO EDU - ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION
# https://github.com/Roberdan/ConvergioEdu
#
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in your values
# Contact: roberdan@fightthestroke.org
# ============================================================================

# ============================================================================
# AI PROVIDERS (Choose one for chat, Azure required for voice)
# ============================================================================

# Option 1: Azure OpenAI (Recommended for production)
# All Azure endpoints must be configured for full functionality
AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o
AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION=2024-08-01-preview

# Voice Features (Azure OpenAI Realtime - REQUIRED for voice)
# Voice will not work without these - text chat fallback will be shown
#
# Available models (Dec 2025):
#   - gpt-realtime (2025-08-28) - GA, recommended, best quality
#   - gpt-realtime-mini (2025-12-15) - GA, faster, lower cost
#   - gpt-4o-realtime-preview (deprecated) - do NOT use
#
# Create deployment: az cognitiveservices account deployment create \
#   --name your-resource --resource-group your-rg \
#   --deployment-name gpt-realtime --model-name gpt-realtime --model-version 2025-08-28 \
#   --sku-name GlobalStandard --sku-capacity 1
#
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-realtime

# Option 2: Ollama (100% local, free, chat/text only)
# If Azure is not configured, the app will automatically try Ollama
# Voice features will NOT work with Ollama (realtime audio not supported)
OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2

# ============================================================================
# AZURE COST MANAGEMENT (Optional - for cost tracking in Settings)
# ============================================================================

# To enable cost tracking, you need a Service Principal with "Cost Management Reader" role:
#
# 1. Create App Registration in Azure AD:
#    az ad app create --display-name "Convergio Cost Reader"
#
# 2. Create Service Principal:
#    az ad sp create --id <app-id>
#
# 3. Create client secret:
#    az ad app credential reset --id <app-id>
#
# 4. Assign "Cost Management Reader" role on subscription:
#    az role assignment create \
#      --assignee <service-principal-id> \
#      --role "Cost Management Reader" \
#      --scope /subscriptions/<subscription-id>

AZURE_TENANT_ID=your-tenant-id
AZURE_CLIENT_ID=your-service-principal-client-id
AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-service-principal-secret
AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=your-subscription-id

# ============================================================================
# DATABASE
# ============================================================================

# SQLite for local development (default)
DATABASE_URL="file:./prisma/dev.db"

# PostgreSQL for production (uncomment and configure when deploying)
# DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@host:5432/convergio?sslmode=require"

# ============================================================================
# GOOGLE OAUTH (For Calendar/Classroom sync)
# ============================================================================

# Use the same Google Cloud project as MirrorBuddy!
# Just add a new OAuth client: APIs & Services > Credentials > Create > OAuth client ID
# Type: "Web application"
# Authorized redirect URIs: http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/google
#                          https://your-domain.com/api/auth/callback/google

GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-web-client-id.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-web-client-secret

# ============================================================================
# PROVIDERS THAT ARE NEVER USED (listed for clarity)
# ============================================================================

# OPENAI_API_KEY=xxx        # DO NOT USE - Use Azure OpenAI instead
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=xxx     # DO NOT USE - Never use Anthropic

# ============================================================================
# DEPLOYMENT NOTES
# ============================================================================
#
# 1. For Vercel deployment:
#    - Add these variables in Project Settings > Environment Variables
#    - No need to upload .env.local file
#
# 2. For Docker deployment:
#    - Pass variables via --env-file or -e flags
#    - Example: docker run --env-file .env.local convergio-web
#
# 3. For local development:
#    - Copy this file: cp .env.example .env.local
#    - Fill in your Azure OpenAI credentials
#    - Run: npm run dev
#
# ============================================================================
# OLLAMA QUICK START (100% local, no cloud, chat only)
# ============================================================================
#
#   # Install Ollama
#   brew install ollama
#
#   # Start server (keep running in terminal)
#   ollama serve
#
#   # Pull a model (in another terminal)
#   ollama pull llama3.2        # Fast, good for education (~2GB)
#   # OR
#   ollama pull mistral         # Alternative (~4GB)
#   # OR
#   ollama pull llama3.1:70b    # Best quality (needs 64GB+ RAM)
#
#   # Then just run the webapp - it will auto-detect Ollama
#   # Note: Voice features require Azure OpenAI Realtime
#
# ============================================================================

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I understand the architecture. Let me analyze what I need to do:

1. The realtime proxy (`src/server/realtime-proxy.ts`) currently reads `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT` and uses it directly for ALL connections.

2. I need to:
   - Add a new env var `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI` to `.env.example`
   - Modify the realtime proxy to accept a deployment parameter from the client
   - Modify the logic to choose between mini and premium models based on character type

But wait, looking at the code more carefully:
- The `realtime-proxy.ts` gets the maestroId from the URL query params (line 123)
- But it doesn't have any logic to determine what deployment to use based on character type
- The deployment is set globally in `getProviderConfig()` (line 35)

I need to:
1. Pass character type information to the proxy (either via query params or another mechanism)
2. Modify `getProviderConfig()` to accept a deployment parameter
3. Use mini by default, but use premium for buddies (MirrorBuddy)

Let me check how the client connects to the proxy to understand what information is available.

- Otherwise, use the mini model

content.thinking:
Great! Now I have a clear picture:

1. `CharacterType` is defined as `'maestro' | 'coach' | 'buddy'` 
2. The character chat view receives `characterType` as a prop
3. I need to pass this information to the WebSocket connection so the proxy can choose the right deployment

Now let me implement the solution:

1. Add env var `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI` to `.env.example`
2. Modify the WebSocket URL to include characterType as a query parameter
3. Modify the realtime proxy to:
   - Read the new env var
   - Extract characterType from the query params
   - Use premium model for 'buddy', mini model for everyone else

Let me start implementing:

I can see that the WebSocket URL is built at line 1067: `wsUrl = ${protocol}://${host}:${proxyPort}?maestroId=${maestro.id}`;

But wait, I need to figure out how to determine the characterType. The maestro object is passed to the connect function, but it doesn't have a characterType field. 

Let me check if there's a way to determine the character type from the maestro ID or if I need to pass it separately. Looking at the code flow:
- CharacterChatView has `characterType` prop
- It calls voice session
- But how does it pass the characterType?

Let me check the full signature of the connect function and how it's called.

Now I see the connect function signature: `const connect = useCallback(async (maestro: Maestro, connectionInfo: ConnectionInfo) => {`

So I need to either:
1. Add characterType to the ConnectionInfo interface
2. OR add a characterType field to the Maestro interface
3. OR determine characterType from the maestro.id

Looking at the code, I think the best approach is to add characterType to ConnectionInfo since that's what gets passed from the UI components. Let me check how it's called in the CharacterChatView.

1. Add `characterType` to the `ConnectionInfo` interface
2. Pass `character.type` when calling `connect()`
3. Include `characterType` in the WebSocket URL query params
4. Modify the proxy to read `characterType` and choose the right deployment

input.old_string:
# Voice Features (Azure OpenAI Realtime - REQUIRED for voice)
# Voice will not work without these - text chat fallback will be shown
#
# Available models (Dec 2025):
#   - gpt-realtime (2025-08-28) - GA, recommended, best quality
#   - gpt-realtime-mini (2025-12-15) - GA, faster, lower cost
#   - gpt-4o-realtime-preview (deprecated) - do NOT use
#
# Create deployment: az cognitiveservices account deployment create \
#   --name your-resource --resource-group your-rg \
#   --deployment-name gpt-realtime --model-name gpt-realtime --model-version 2025-08-28 \
#   --sku-name GlobalStandard --sku-capacity 1
#
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-realtime
input.new_string:
# Voice Features (Azure OpenAI Realtime - REQUIRED for voice)
# Voice will not work without these - text chat fallback will be shown
#
# Available models (Dec 2025):
#   - gpt-realtime (2025-08-28) - GA, recommended, best quality
#   - gpt-realtime-mini (2025-12-15) - GA, faster, lower cost
#   - gpt-4o-realtime-preview (deprecated) - do NOT use
#
# Create deployment: az cognitiveservices account deployment create \
#   --name your-resource --resource-group your-rg \
#   --deployment-name gpt-realtime --model-name gpt-realtime --model-version 2025-08-28 \
#   --sku-name GlobalStandard --sku-capacity 1
#
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-realtime

# Cost Optimization: Use mini model by default, premium for MirrorBuddy only
# Mini model deployment (80-90% cheaper, good quality for most use cases)
# Premium model (AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT) will be used ONLY for MirrorBuddy
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI=gpt-4o-mini-realtime

#
# Available models (Dec 2025):

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ============================================================================
# CONVERGIO EDU - ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION
# https://github.com/Roberdan/ConvergioEdu
#
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in your values
# Contact: roberdan@fightthestroke.org
# ============================================================================

# ============================================================================
# AI PROVIDERS (Choose one for chat, Azure required for voice)
# ============================================================================

# Option 1: Azure OpenAI (Recommended for production)
# All Azure endpoints must be configured for full functionality
AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_CHAT_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-4o
AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION=2024-08-01-preview

# Voice Features (Azure OpenAI Realtime - REQUIRED for voice)
# Voice will not work without these - text chat fallback will be shown
#
# Available models (Dec 2025):
#   - gpt-realtime (2025-08-28) - GA, recommended, best quality
#   - gpt-realtime-mini (2025-12-15) - GA, faster, lower cost
#   - gpt-4o-realtime-preview (deprecated) - do NOT use
#
# Create deployment: az cognitiveservices account deployment create \
#   --name your-resource --resource-group your-rg \
#   --deployment-name gpt-realtime --model-name gpt-realtime --model-version 2025-08-28 \
#   --sku-name GlobalStandard --sku-capacity 1
#
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_ENDPOINT=https://your-resource.openai.azure.com
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_API_KEY=your-api-key
AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT=gpt-realtime

# Option 2: Ollama (100% local, free, chat/text only)
# If Azure is not configured, the app will automatically try Ollama
# Voice features will NOT work with Ollama (realtime audio not supported)
OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434
OLLAMA_MODEL=llama3.2

# ============================================================================
# AZURE COST MANAGEMENT (Optional - for cost tracking in Settings)
# ============================================================================

# To enable cost tracking, you need a Service Principal with "Cost Management Reader" role:
#
# 1. Create App Registration in Azure AD:
#    az ad app create --display-name "Convergio Cost Reader"
#
# 2. Create Service Principal:
#    az ad sp create --id <app-id>
#
# 3. Create client secret:
#    az ad app credential reset --id <app-id>
#
# 4. Assign "Cost Management Reader" role on subscription:
#    az role assignment create \
#      --assignee <service-principal-id> \
#      --role "Cost Management Reader" \
#      --scope /subscriptions/<subscription-id>

AZURE_TENANT_ID=your-tenant-id
AZURE_CLIENT_ID=your-service-principal-client-id
AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-service-principal-secret
AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=your-subscription-id

# ============================================================================
# DATABASE
# ============================================================================

# SQLite for local development (default)
DATABASE_URL="file:./prisma/dev.db"

# PostgreSQL for production (uncomment and configure when deploying)
# DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@host:5432/convergio?sslmode=require"

# ============================================================================
# GOOGLE OAUTH (For Calendar/Classroom sync)
# ============================================================================

# Use the same Google Cloud project as MirrorBuddy!
# Just add a new OAuth client: APIs & Services > Credentials > Create > OAuth client ID
# Type: "Web application"
# Authorized redirect URIs: http://localhost:3000/api/auth/callback/google
#                          https://your-domain.com/api/auth/callback/google

GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-web-client-id.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=your-web-client-secret

# ============================================================================
# PROVIDERS THAT ARE NEVER USED (listed for clarity)
# ============================================================================

# OPENAI_API_KEY=xxx        # DO NOT USE - Use Azure OpenAI instead
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=xxx     # DO NOT USE - Never use Anthropic

# ============================================================================
# DEPLOYMENT NOTES
# ============================================================================
#
# 1. For Vercel deployment:
#    - Add these variables in Project Settings > Environment Variables
#    - No need to upload .env.local file
#
# 2. For Docker deployment:
#    - Pass variables via --env-file or -e flags
#    - Example: docker run --env-file .env.local convergio-web
#
# 3. For local development:
#    - Copy this file: cp .env.example .env.local
#    - Fill in your Azure OpenAI credentials
#    - Run: npm run dev
#
# ============================================================================
# OLLAMA QUICK START (100% local, no cloud, chat only)
# ============================================================================
#
#   # Install Ollama
#   brew install ollama
#
#   # Start server (keep running in terminal)
#   ollama serve
#
#   # Pull a model (in another terminal)
#   ollama pull llama3.2        # Fast, good for education (~2GB)
#   # OR
#   ollama pull mistral         # Alternative (~4GB)
#   # OR
#   ollama pull llama3.1:70b    # Best quality (needs 64GB+ RAM)
#
#   # Then just run the webapp - it will auto-detect Ollama
#   # Note: Voice features require Azure OpenAI Realtime
#
# ============================================================================

# Option 2: Ollama (100% local, free, chat/text only)
# If Azure is not configured, the app will automatically try Ollama
# Voice features will NOT work with Ollama (realtime audio not supported)

content.content:
The file /Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/server/realtime-proxy.ts has been updated. Here's the result of running `cat -n` on a snippet of the edited file:
    40→  // MirrorBuddy (buddy type) needs premium model for emotional detection quality
    41→  const usePremium = characterType === 'buddy';
    42→  const azureDeployment = usePremium ? azureDeploymentPremium : (azureDeploymentMini || azureDeploymentPremium);
    43→
    44→  if (azureEndpoint && azureApiKey && azureDeployment) {
    45→    const normalized = azureEndpoint
    46→      .replace(/^https:\/\//, 'wss://')
    47→      .replace(/^http:\/\//, 'ws://');
    48→    const url = new URL(normalized);
    49→
    50→    // Log which deployment is being used
    51→    const modelTier = usePremium ? 'PREMIUM' : 'MINI';
    52→    logger.debug(`Using ${modelTier} deployment: ${azureDeployment} for characterType: ${characterType}`);
    53→
    54→    // =========================================================================
    55→    // AZURE REALTIME API: Preview vs GA
    56→    // =========================================================================
    57→    // CRITICAL: Azure has TWO different API formats that use different:
    58→    //   1. URL paths
    59→    //   2. Query parameters
    60→    //   3. Event names (response.audio.delta vs response.output_audio.delta)
    61→    //
    62→    // Preview API (gpt-4o-realtime-preview):
    63→    //   - Path: /openai/realtime
    64→    //   - Events: response.audio.delta, response.audio_transcript.delta
    65→    //
    66→    // GA API (gpt-realtime):
    67→    //   - Path: /openai/v1/realtime
    68→    //   - Events: response.output_audio.delta, response.output_audio_transcript.delta
    69→    //
    70→    // See: docs/AZURE_REALTIME_API.md for full documentation
    71→    // =========================================================================
    72→    const isPreviewModel = azureDeployment.includes('4o') || azureDeployment.includes('preview');
    73→
    74→    if (isPreviewModel) {
    75→      // Preview API format: /openai/realtime with api-version and deployment
    76→      url.pathname = '/openai/realtime';

content.text:
## WAVE 1 Voice Model Migration - COMPLETED

I've successfully implemented the voice model migration from gpt-4o-realtime to gpt-realtime-mini for 80-90% cost savings. Here's what was done:

### Changes Made

**1. Environment Configuration** (`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/.env.example`)
- Added `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI` environment variable
- Set default value to `gpt-4o-mini-realtime`
- Added documentation explaining the cost optimization strategy

**2. Type Definitions** (`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/hooks/use-voice-session.ts`)
- Updated `ConnectionInfo` interface to include optional `characterType` field
- Supports values: `'maestro' | 'coach' | 'buddy'`

**3. WebSocket Connection** (`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/lib/hooks/use-voice-session.ts`)
- Modified WebSocket URL construction to include `characterType` query parameter
- Defaults to `'maestro'` if not specified
- Example URL: `ws://localhost:3001?maestroId=prof-fibonacci&characterType=buddy`

**4. Character Voice Integration** (`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/components/conversation/components/voice-call-overlay.tsx`)
- Updated to pass `character.type` when calling `connect()`
- Ensures correct characterType is sent for buddies, coaches, and maestros

**5. Realtime Proxy Logic** (`/Users/roberdan/GitHub/ConvergioEdu/src/server/realtime-proxy.ts`)
- Added `CharacterType` type definition
- Modified `getProviderConfig()` to accept `characterType` parameter
- Implemented smart deployment selection:
  - **MirrorBuddy (buddy)**: Uses `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT` (premium model) for high-quality emotional detection
  - **Maestros & Coaches**: Uses `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI` (mini model) for 80-90% cost savings
  - Falls back to premium if mini is not configured
- Added logging to show which deployment tier is being used
- Extracts `characterType` from WebSocket query parameters

### Cost Optimization Strategy

```
┌─────────────────┬──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┐
│ Character Type  │ Model Used   │ Reason                          │
├─────────────────┼──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┤
│ Buddy           │ PREMIUM      │ Emotional detection quality     │
│ Coach           │ MINI         │ 80-90% cost savings             │
│ Maestro         │ MINI         │ 80-90% cost savings             │
└─────────────────┴──────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Verification

- **TypeScript**: ✅ No type errors
- **Lint**: ⚠️ Pre-existing errors unrelated to this change
- **Backwards Compatible**: ✅ Defaults to 'maestro' if characterType not provided
- **Fallback Safe**: ✅ Uses premium model if mini not configured

### Next Steps (Deployment)

To activate this feature in production:

1. Set `AZURE_OPENAI_REALTIME_DEPLOYMENT_MINI=gpt-4o-mini-realtime` in your environment
2. The system will automatically:
   - Use mini model for all maestros and coaches
   - Use premium model only for MirrorBuddy
   - Log which deployment is being used for monitoring

All code changes are complete and type-safe. Ready for testing and deployment.
